analysis_result: 13q loss was observed in 5 of 8 MPNST-G1 tumors (62.5%).
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST-G1 tumors (all samples annotated Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' in Supplementary Data 1 / MOESM4 Clinical Data sheet: M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803)
assumptions_made: CONUMEE R/Bioconductor package was not installable (minfi/DNAcopy Fortran build failure; gfortran unavailable); implemented a homemade CONUMEE-like pipeline in Python following CONUMEE's core methodology; total probe intensity computed from raw IDATs (methylprep IdatDataset) as sum of Methylated+Unmethylated signal (Type I: Grn[addrA]+Grn[addrB] or Red[addrA]+Red[addrB]; Type II: Grn[addrA]+Red[addrA]); per-sample intensity normalized by sample median; 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples used as CNV-neutral reference (matching the paper's diploid-like reference group); log2 ratio per probe = log2(sample_norm / median(reference_norm)); probes filtered to autosomal CpG probes with hg19 coordinates from the EPIC manifest; arm-level log2 ratio computed as median across 20,997 probes on chromosome 13q (chr13 is acrocentric so p-arm is non-informative); 13q loss defined as arm-median log2 ratio < -0.10, which is CONUMEE's conventional loss threshold and also equals the noise-based threshold (reference mean - 3SD = -0.102) in this dataset; MPNST-G1 values of -0.116, -0.244, -0.234, -0.153, -0.501 exceed threshold (losses); values +0.089, +0.169, +0.063 are near-diploid; comparator MPNST-G2 showed 3/8 (37.5%) 13q loss by the same pipeline, consistent with G1 being the more chromosomally unstable subgroup.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx 'Clinical Data' sheet: 108 samples (Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, Sentrix_ID, ...).
   - GSE207207_idats/: 216 IDAT files (108 paired Grn+Red) covering all 108 Sentrix_IDs + EPIC manifest GPL21145 (867,926 probes).
   - No missing Sentrix_IDs; every sample has a complete IDAT pair.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter: Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' (exact match on underscored label) -> 8 samples (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803).
   - Final analytic cohort: 8 MPNST-G1 tumors. Reference cohort for CNV ratios: 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples (expected CNV-neutral).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Raw IDATs decompressed to temp files and parsed with methylprep.files.IdatDataset (GREEN and RED channels).
   - Total intensity per probe = M + U channel intensities, computed per Infinium design:
     * Type I probes: Grn[AddressA]+Grn[AddressB] (if Color_Channel=='Grn') or Red[AddressA]+Red[AddressB] (if 'Red').
     * Type II probes: Grn[AddressA]+Red[AddressA].
   - Probes restricted to cg/ch CpG probes with Genome_Build hg19 CHR + MAPINFO; kept autosomes (chr1-22) with all-positive intensity across 108 samples: 844,537 probes.
   - Chromosome-arm assignment via hg19 centromere midpoints (chr13 centromere ~17.9 Mb; chr13 is acrocentric so only 13q is informative, 20,997 probes).

D. COMPUTATION
   - Per-sample normalization: intensity / sample median intensity.
   - Reference = median across 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples at each probe.
   - log2 ratio = log2(sample_norm / reference_median) per probe.
   - Arm-level log2r = median log2r across probes within the arm.
   - 13q loss call: arm-median log2r < -0.10. This equals both CONUMEE's conventional amplification/loss threshold and the noise-based threshold (reference mean - 3*SD = -0.102).

E. RESULT
   Per-sample 13q arm-median log2 ratios in MPNST-G1:
     M1014: -0.116  (LOSS)
     M2252: -0.244  (LOSS)
     M2346: -0.234  (LOSS)
     M2372: -0.153  (LOSS)
     M3048: -0.501  (LOSS, deepest)
     M3398: +0.089  (no loss)
     M6712: +0.169  (no loss)
     M803:  +0.063  (no loss)
   13q LOSS FREQUENCY IN MPNST-G1: 5 / 8 = 62.5%
   Reference (Benign_NF-G4) 13q log2r: mean -0.000, SD 0.034, range [-0.051, +0.089] - confirms reference is diploid and the -0.10 threshold is >3 SDs below reference mean.
   Comparator: MPNST-G2 13q loss = 3 / 8 = 37.5% (M1677, M2325, M9450), consistent with G1 being more genomically unstable than G2 per the paper's subgroup definitions.

F. CAVEATS
   - CONUMEE (and its dependencies minfi/DNAcopy) were not installable due to missing gfortran; therefore a homemade Python re-implementation of CONUMEE's total-intensity/log2-ratio methodology was used. This does not include CONUMEE's circular binary segmentation (DNAcopy), so segment-level (sub-arm) calls are not produced; arm-level medians are substituted.
   - Benign_NF-G4 (n=20) was chosen as the CNV-neutral reference since benign neurofibromas are the closest-to-diploid tissue in this cohort; CONUMEE's recommended practice is to use a set of presumably-balanced samples as reference, which these serve. Using a different reference (e.g., dermal NFs) would shift baseline but is unlikely to change 13q calls given the magnitude of the observed losses (-0.12 to -0.50).
   - Threshold of |log2r| > 0.10 is CONUMEE's conventional call cutoff; in this dataset it coincides with reference mean - 3SD (= -0.102), giving identical 62.5% result. Loosening the threshold to -0.05 would call 5/8 (unchanged, since 3 non-loss samples are all >+0.05); tightening to -0.15 would still call 4/8 (50%) - but 62.5% is the value under the standard threshold.
   - chr13 is acrocentric: p-arm is heterochromatic and has essentially no CpG probes, so only 13q was assessed (standard practice).
   - Homemade pipeline lacks CONUMEE's spline-based background correction and GC-content bias fitting. Residual technical noise in reference is low (SD=0.034), suggesting bias is minor.
   - Answer represents frequency; no confidence interval requested but exact 95% Wilson CI for 5/8 is 30.6%-86.3%.
